summary: Client-Centered Architecture Final Verification Complete - All 36/36 tests passed. Verified client data model, provider/mom appointments, visits, notes, contracts, invoices, active client logic (6 weeks rule), and role-based access control.
critical_code_review_comments: Client data model verified: All clients have client_id, provider_id, is_active fields
Client detail has _counts object with appointments, notes, contracts, invoices, visits counts
Client timeline returns properly structured items with type, id, date, title fields
Active client filtering works correctly - include_inactive=false filters out inactive clients
Provider dashboard returns active_clients count alongside total_clients and upcoming_appointments
Midwife dashboard has role-specific stats: visits_this_month, births_this_month, prenatal_clients
Provider appointments correctly require client_id and store provider_id
Mom appointments endpoint works correctly at /api/mom/appointments
Midwife visits correctly require client_id and auto-create linked appointments
Doula invoices have client_id and provider_id fields
Midwife invoices have client_id and provider_id fields
Role-based access control verified: DOULA and MIDWIFE can access /api/provider/* routes
Role-based access control verified: MOM blocked from /api/provider/* routes (403)
Role-based access control verified: Providers blocked from mom-only routes (403)
No MongoDB ObjectId serialization errors in any response
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_consultation appointments and visits during testing, cleaned up after execution
context_for_next_testing_agent: Final verification of client-centered architecture complete. All data model fields verified: client_id, provider_id, is_active present on clients; appointments and visits have client_id and provider_id; contracts and invoices have client_id and provider_id. The 6-week active client rule is working via is_client_active utility. Role-based access control properly enforced - MOM cannot access /api/provider/* routes and providers cannot access mom-only routes like /api/birth-plan.

details: GET /api/provider/clients returns clients with client_id, provider_id, is_active
GET /api/provider/clients/{id} returns _counts (appointments, notes, contracts, invoices, visits)
GET /api/provider/clients/{id}/timeline returns structured timeline items with type, id, date, title
include_inactive=false filters to active clients only
Midwife dashboard has visits_this_month, births_this_month, prenatal_clients
GET /api/provider/appointments returns appointments with client_id, provider_id
GET /api/provider/notes returns notes with client_id, provider_id
GET /api/provider/visits returns visits with client_id, provider_id/midwife_id
Created visits automatically create linked appointments
GET /api/doula/invoices returns invoices with client_id, provider_id
GET /api/midwife/invoices returns invoices with client_id, provider_id
MIDWIFE can access all /api/provider/* routes including /api/provider/visits (200)
Providers blocked from mom-only routes like /api/birth-plan, /api/timeline (403)

mocked_apis.note: All tested routes use real database queries. Client-centered data model verified with live MongoDB.
